era_context: Identical to -2345 and -2346: Old Kingdom Egypt (Dynasty VI) under Pepi II, Akkadian Empire under Naram-Sin, Indus Valley Civilization in mature phase. This is a period of established empires and civilizations, before the major disruptions of the late 23rd century BCE.

events.title: Egypt under Pepi II: administrative continuity
events.description: Pepi II's reign continues with no documented specific events for this year. The Egyptian state maintains its administrative apparatus, with provincial governors managing local affairs and the central government collecting taxes and provisioning temples. The gradual decentralization of power that will eventually lead to the First Intermediate Period is underway but not yet acute.

sources.name: Administrative papyri and tomb inscriptions from Dynasty VI

events.certainty_note: No specific events are documented for -2347. The description reflects the general state of Egypt during Pepi II's reign.

events.title: Akkadian Empire: imperial consolidation under Naram-Sin
events.description: Naram-Sin's reign continues with ongoing military and administrative activities. The Akkadian Empire is at its territorial maximum, controlling much of Mesopotamia, the Levant, and parts of Anatolia. No specific events are documented for this year, but the empire's administrative system is functioning at full capacity.

sources.name: Akkadian royal inscriptions and administrative records

events.certainty_note: Naram-Sin's reign is well-attested, but no specific events are documented for -2347.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are documented for -2347, making it impossible to identify disconfirming evidence.
historiographic_note: The year -2347 is not individually documented in surviving sources. Historical understanding of this period is based on broader patterns and general characterizations of the era.
